channel.title: SSbusinessAndMarket
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SSbusinessAndMarket, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: I debated the father of open source for 2 years. Now the AI battle is the same, only bigger
item.description: Two Sigma co-founder David Siegel warns that restricting AI mirrors the software industry's near-mistake in the 1980s, but with greater consequences.

item.title: YouTube's founders split $650 million in 2006 sale to Google; holding out could have netted a share of $550 billion
item.description: YouTube's cofounders could have joined the billionaire club alongside Google's Sundar Pichai if they had retained the business, which has grown 333 times since.

item.title: Wall Street analysts increasingly doubt U.S. government's 'misleading' job numbers
item.description: Jamie Cox of Harris Financial Group says, 'These data are misleading and should be disregarded.'

item.title: AI replacing human interaction in the workplace, leadership expert warns
item.description: Leadership expert Leena Rinne says the issue is not AI itself but the loss of social connection and emotional intelligence.
